description: Amendment policy governing how constitutional and governance documents may be changed. Preserves Reality, Selection, and Governed Recursion. No automation may self-approve constitutional changes.

reality.label: Reality before judgment
reality.rule: Amendments must be grounded in observed behavior, documented facts, or validated failure modes — not hypothetical needs.
reality.enforcement: PR body must cite evidence (issue, incident, test failure, or doc inconsistency) justifying the change.

selection.label: Selection before memory
selection.rule: Amendments must preserve existing invariants unless the PR explicitly identifies the invariant as obsolete and provides replacement reasoning.
selection.enforcement: Removing or weakening a rule requires a Non-Goals section entry explaining why the rule is no longer needed.

governedRecursion.label: Governed recursion
governedRecursion.rule: No amendment may grant the constitution steward or any worker the ability to self-approve future amendments.
governedRecursion.enforcement: The amendment policy itself and the seed constitution are immutable by automation. Changes require human-authored PRs.

scope.description: Files subject to amendment governance rules.

requireHumanAuthorship.description: Amendment PRs must not be generated by automation. The PR author must be a human account.
requireHumanAuthorship.enforcement: PR body must not contain automation-generated markers without a human sign-off.

requireArchitectReview.description: Amendments to governance documents require review from architecture-review role at minimum.
requireArchitectReview.enforcement: PR cannot merge without approval from at least one required role.

requireRepoOwnerApproval.description: Constitutional amendments require final approval from the repository owner.
requireRepoOwnerApproval.enforcement: PR cannot merge without repo-owner approval.

requireDocsMirrorSync.description: Changes to .github/ai-policy/ files must include a corresponding update to the docs mirror if one exists.
requireDocsMirrorSync.enforcement: Docs authority guard checks for corresponding mirror updates.

requireEvidenceInPRBody.description: Amendment PRs must pass the full PR handoff guard with all seven sections.

mergeEligibility.reason: Constitutional amendments are high-risk governance changes that always require human review and approval.

conflictGroup.name: constitution-amendment
conflictGroup.description: Amendment tasks share a single conflict group to prevent concurrent amendments from colliding.
conflictGroup.rule: Only one amendment task may be in-flight at a time across all governance paths.

budgets.rationale: Amendments should be small, focused, and independently reviewable. Large changes should be split into sequential amendments.

constitutionSteward.description: Constitution steward may propose amendments but cannot approve or merge them. Steward cannot expand its own scope.

aiWorker.description: Regular AI workers cannot propose, approve, or merge constitutional amendments.

humanOwner.description: Only the human owner may approve and merge constitutional amendments.

rollback.description: If an amendment introduces a governance conflict or breaks validation, the amendment PR must be reverted.
triggerConditions: npm run check fails after merge
Conflict with existing invariants in seed-constitution.md
rollback.process: Revert the amendment PR. Open a new issue documenting the conflict. The constitution steward investigates before re-proposing.

escapeHatch.description: If the amendment process itself is blocking critical work, the repository owner may bypass with explicit written approval.
conditions: Repository owner explicitly approves bypass in the PR or issue
Bypass is documented in the PR body
A follow-up issue is opened to reconcile the bypass with the amendment policy
